# Pablo Albaladejo

> Personal site of Pablo Albaladejo (sometimes misspelled "Albadalejo"),
> AI Engineer — AWS, TypeScript, 17+ years in SaaS, based in Madrid,
> Spain. Builds production software with AI agents. Founder of Deepgent,
> the multi-tenant AI chatbot SaaS. Creator of Kaiord, the open-source,
> local-first training platform.

## Pages

- [Home](https://pabloalbaladejo.com/): positioning and projects, in English
- [Talks](https://pabloalbaladejo.com/talks): speaker bio, talk abstracts,
  and booking (agentic development, LLM evals, GEO), in English
- [CV](https://pabloalbaladejo.com/cv): curriculum vitae — experience, 9x AWS
  certifications, education, and PDF download, in English

## Writing

- [Evaluating LLM outputs in production: 22 benchmarks, a 90% gate](https://pabloalbaladejo.com/blog/evaluating-llm-outputs-in-production):
  how I evaluate LLM structured output with a 22-benchmark eval harness,
  tiered assertions, and a hard 90% CI gate
- [Shipping production software with AI agents](https://pabloalbaladejo.com/blog/shipping-production-software-with-ai-agents):
  the system that lets AI agents ship production code — specs before code,
  zero-tolerance CI as the reviewer, mechanical convention guards, and
  isolated worktrees, grounded in the AI-built Kaiord codebase
- [Observability for LLM pipelines: from streams to sinks](https://pabloalbaladejo.com/blog/observability-for-llm-pipelines):
  what observability means for LLM pipelines and why classic APM misses it —
  deferred-flush streaming metrics, telemetry as a swappable sink (with real
  code), and a pragmatic adoption path
- [GEO: how AI agents find you — a same-day case study](https://pabloalbaladejo.com/blog/geo-how-ai-agents-find-you):
  a mechanism-by-mechanism case study of optimizing kaiord.com for AI answer
  engines in one day — llms.txt, robots rules for AI crawlers, structured
  data, Markdown mirrors, and registries — with a link to every artifact
- [Stop Chatting, Start Specifying: Spec-Driven Design with Kiro IDE](https://dev.to/kirodotdev/stop-chatting-start-specifying-spec-driven-design-with-kiro-ide-3b3o):
  spec-driven development with Kiro IDE, using the Kaiord project as the case
  study — external, on the Kiro blog (dev.to), 2025-12-01
- [Observable AI Streaming on AWS — a 4-part series](https://dev.to/pabloalbaladejo/observable-ai-streaming-on-aws-part-1-api-gateway-rest-with-lambda-595a):
  streaming LLM responses from Bedrock through Lambda with deferred observability
  via TransformStream flush() — external, dev.to, 2025-02
- [RSS feed](https://pabloalbaladejo.com/rss.xml)

## Projects

- [Kaiord](https://kaiord.com): open-source, local-first training platform
  (calendar, AI assistant, Garmin & WHOOP sync, TypeScript SDK, CLI, MCP
  server). Docs corpus for LLMs: https://kaiord.com/docs/llms-full.txt
- [Deepgent](https://deepgent.net): multi-tenant SaaS platform giving
  companies a branded AI chatbot over their own curated knowledge base
  (embeddable widget, self-service portal, RAG on AWS Bedrock)
- [trainingpeaks-sdk](https://github.com/pablo-albaladejo/trainingpeaks-sdk):
  TypeScript SDK for the TrainingPeaks platform
- [streaming-lambda-ai-sdk](https://github.com/pablo-albaladejo/streaming-lambda-ai-sdk):
  structured LLM responses from Amazon Bedrock streamed through AWS Lambda
- [tanita-to-garmin-cdk](https://github.com/pablo-albaladejo/tanita-to-garmin-cdk):
  serverless AWS app syncing body-composition data into Garmin Connect
